channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: US to designate two Brazilian gangs as ‘terrorist’ organisations
item.description: Trump administration has used crime and drug trafficking to push for greater US military influence across Latin America.

item.title: Iran war live: Tehran, Trump yet to comment on 60-day truce extension plan
item.description: Lebanese PM Nawaf Salam says 'nothing can justify' Israel's military onslaught against 'peaceful people' in Lebanon.

item.title: Netanyahu orders Israeli army to seize 70 percent of Gaza
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says he has ordered the military to take control of 70 percent of Gaza.

item.title: Iran ambassador hits out at ‘country to the north’ in US World Cup visa row
item.description: Iran's football team still lacks US visas and is not competing on 'equal terms', Tehran's envoy to Mexico says.

item.title: Tense protests erupt outside Delaney Hall immigrant detention centre in US
item.description: A hunger strike at the New Jersey facility has raised questions about conditions in US immigration detention centres.

item.title: PSG vs Arsenal: UEFA Champions League final – 10 things to know
item.description: Al Jazeera runs you through this season's UEFA Champions League final between Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal.

item.title: Indians celebrate Eid al-Adha amid prayer restrictions
item.description: Indians celebrate Eid al-Adha amid prayer restrictions.

item.title: Ireland and Qatar halted twice by protests against upcoming Israel fixtures
item.description: Republic of Ireland are set to face Israel twice in UEFA Nations League matches, but are facing calls for a boycott.

item.title: Colombia’s first left-wing gov’t has reduced poverty, but has pile of debt
item.description: A big challenge for Colombia's next president will be how to reduce country's debt without sacrificing social gains.

item.title: Guatemala denies agreeing to US strikes against drug traffickers
item.description: President Bernardo Arevalo's government says it requested security cooperation but did not approve US attacks.

item.title: Defiant Lebanese families refuse to leave Tyre after homes bombed
item.description: Families in the Lebanese city of Tyre saw their homes destroyed by Israeli bombs just hours after they were ordered evac

item.title: US judge rejects immediate block on Trump’s mail-in voting executive order
item.description: Judge Carl Nichols says Democrats’ legal challenge is premature as the administration moves to implement the measure.

item.title: Europe hit by record-breaking heatwave
item.description: European countries are issuing heat warnings as an unseasonal heatwave drives record temperatures across the continent.

item.title: Demands grow for closure of US detention centre over inhumane conditions
item.description: A group of US lawmakers are joining protesters in demanding a controversial immigration detention centre be shut down.

item.title: EU sanctions ‘extremist’ Israeli settlers in occupied West Bank
item.description: European Union says sanctioned individuals and groups have violated the rights of Palestinians.

item.title: Trump administration sues states for denying ICE agents undercover plates
item.description: The Department of Justice has argued that states are obliged to give ICE vehicles confidential licence plates.

item.title: Three wounded in Swiss train station stabbing labelled ‘act of terror’
item.description: Swiss authorities arrest suspect who had come to authorities' attention in 2015 for distributing ISIL propaganda.

item.title: What’s behind South Africa’s anti-migrant protests?
item.description: Thousands of foreign workers are under increasing pressure to leave the country.

item.title: Israel’s Netanyahu directs army to seize 70 percent of Gaza Strip
item.description: Israeli army has already expanded its control of Gaza by 11 percent over the 'Yellow Line', beyond terms of 'ceasefire'.

item.title: Rebuilding US weapons stockpile may ‘take years’ post-Iran war
item.description: Restoring pre-war stockpiles of critical US munitions will take at least two years, according to CSIS report.

item.title: US, Iran reach MoU on 60-day truce, Trump’s approval pending
item.description: US and Iranian negotiators have reached a memorandum of understanding on a proposed 60-day ceasefire extension.

item.title: US and Iran reach tentative deal for 60-day truce extension, officials say
item.description: The memorandum of understanding, which would enable further negotiations, still requires Donald Trump's final approval.

item.title: CNN sues Perplexity, alleging unlawful distribution of copyrighted content
item.description: Perplexity unlawfully copied thousands of CNN stories, videos and images to power its products, CNN said in its lawsuit.

item.title: How War Changed Eid in Lebanon
item.description: After nearly three years of war and displacement, Eid in Lebanon no longer feels like celebration for many families.

item.title: UN ‘adds Israel to blacklist’ for conflict-related sexual violence
item.description: Israeli ambassador to the UN says Tel Aviv will cut ties with UN chief Antonio Guterres over the upcoming report.
